campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review the city's finances for July 2025, including a report on how the city's investments performed that month and an update on the treasurer's accounts. The council also discussed progress on a new transportation plan aimed at improving how residents get around Campbell using different modes of travel like buses, biking, and walking.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment performance and cash flow
City treasurer's financial report for July
Campbell Multimodal Transportation Plan development and next steps
Budget and spending review

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public hearing to get community input on a new rule or policy. After hearing from residents, they moved forward by introducing an ordinance (a new city law) and are planning to vote on it at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback
New ordinance introduced (specific topic not detailed in agenda)
Vote scheduled for next regular council meeting
First reading of ordinance waived to speed up process

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from various sessions held in March and to authorize the Town Manager to sign a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The agenda was primarily administrative, focused on approving records from previous meetings and moving forward with a construction project.
keyTopics: Approved multiple sets of meeting minutes from March 2026 sessions
Authorized Town Manager to execute construction contract with QLM, Inc.
Administrative business - no major policy changes or public hearings noted

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 9, 2026
san-jose.summary: The City Council reviewed programs and plans focused on supporting San José's young people, including youth empowerment initiatives and summer safety programs. The meeting also covered code enforcement activities and updates on library facilities and services. Overall, the council discussed how the city is investing in youth services, keeping neighborhoods safe, and improving public spaces.
keyTopics: Youth Empowerment Alliance and summer safety grant programs
Updates on city's master plan for children and youth services
Code enforcement activities and violations
Library building improvements and customer experience

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 7, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026 that included a virtual option for residents who wanted to participate from home. Residents could join online or by phone to listen and speak during public comment periods.
keyTopics: Virtual meeting access via Zoom and phone dial-in
Public comment procedures (raise hand feature or *9 on phone)
Online participation options for residents unable to attend in person

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale Human Relations Commission — April 9, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Human Relations Commission held a meeting on April 9, 2026, where they honored the flag and heard personal stories from three commissioners about their experiences in Sunnyvale. The group also approved the official notes from their previous meeting in March.
keyTopics: Personal stories shared by three Human Relations Commissioners
Approval of March 12, 2026 meeting minutes
Human Relations Commission activities and updates

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 8, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met to discuss several community programs and initiatives. The main topics included a presentation about a food and budget program called Budget and Bites, an introduction to a youth-led organization called Unmuted Tabs, and information about summer job opportunities for young people in the city.
keyTopics: Budget and Bites program presentation
Unmuted Tabs youth-led organization
City of Cupertino Summer Jobs opportunities
Approval of previous meeting minutes from March 11

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 7, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026 using both in-person and online options so residents could participate however works best for them. The council provided multiple ways for the public to join and share their opinions, including Zoom video conferencing and phone dial-in.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format available (in-person and remote via Zoom)
Public can call in by phone at 1(669) 900-6833
Written comments can be submitted through eComment tab on City Council Agenda page
Residents have multiple ways to participate in local government decisions

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met on April 7, 2026, to handle routine business and review funding plans. Key items included updating the city's five-year community development plan, reviewing requests from individual council members for new projects, and considering changes to how the city fills vacant council seats.
keyTopics: Updates to the 2025-2030 community development and funding plan
New project requests from individual council members
Proposed changes to council vacancy procedures
Approval of previous meeting minutes and upcoming meeting calendar

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 31, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council reviewed and approved financial plans for Palo Alto's gas utility service for the next fiscal year. This included adjusting gas rates for residential customers, businesses, and large commercial users, along with moving money between the utility fund and the city's general budget.
keyTopics: Gas rate changes for homes and businesses
Utility budget and financial reserves for 2027
Transfer of funds between gas utility and city general fund
Meeting minutes approval from March 4 utilities commission meeting
